question: What is the maximum reimbursable nightly hotel rate for Tokyo?
chunks: Global Travel & Expense Policy (rev.
All business travel must be booked through the Concur portal at least 14 days in advance where practicable.
Economy class is required for flights under 6 hours; premium economy is permitted for flights of 6 hours or more, and business class requires VP approval regardless of duration.
Lodging: the standard reimbursable hotel rate is capped at $220 per night before taxes.
A higher cap of $260 per night applies to designated high-cost domestic metros (New York, San Francisco, Boston).
For designated high-cost international cities - Tokyo, Zurich, and Singapore - the nightly hotel cap is raised to $340.
Stays beyond 7 consecutive nights require a negotiated corporate rate.
Meals are reimbursed on a per-diem basis: $75/day domestic, $90/day international, reduced by $20 for any day where a conference provides lunch.
Alcohol is not reimbursable except at client-hosted dinners with prior manager approval.
Ground transport: ride-share and taxis are reimbursable; rental cars require a documented business need and must be compact or mid-size class.
Personal mileage is reimbursed at $0.67/mile.
Receipts are mandatory for any single expense above $25.
Expense reports must be submitted within 30 days of trip completion; reports filed after 60 days will not be reimbursed absent a written exception from Finance.

question: What is the requests-per-minute limit for the Enterprise tier on the /export endpoint?
chunks: Acme API - Rate Limits & Quotas (v3)
Rate limits are enforced per API key using a sliding window.
The global per-minute request ceilings by plan are: Free - 10 requests/minute; Pro - 120 requests/minute; Business - 300 requests/minute; Enterprise - 600 requests/minute.
Bursts up to 2x the limit are tolerated for up to 5 seconds before 429 responses begin.
Most endpoints draw from the global per-key limit.
However, several compute-heavy endpoints have their own independent caps that override the plan limit.
The /search endpoint is capped at 60 requests/minute on all tiers.
The /export endpoint is rate-limited separately to 30 requests/minute on every paid tier, including Enterprise, regardless of the global plan limit; Free keys cannot call /export at all.
The /webhooks/test endpoint is capped at 5 requests/minute.
Daily quotas also apply: Free - 1,000 calls/day; Pro - 50,000; Business - 250,000; Enterprise - unmetered.
Exceeding the daily quota returns HTTP 403 with quota_exceeded.
Rate-limit headers are returned on every response.
Customers needing higher limits may request a custom contract via their account manager.

question: Was the mobile app v2 launch approved for Q3?
answer: No (deferred, conditional on the accessibility audit by Sept 5)
chunks: Product Sync - Notes (Aug 12)
Attendees: Priya (PM), Marco (Eng), Dana (Design), Sam (QA).
Budget: The additional $150K for the infra migration was approved.
Marco to proceed with the vendor contract this week.
Hiring: Two backend roles approved; Dana's request for a second designer was approved contingent on the role being backfilled internally first.
Mobile app v2 launch: Extended discussion.
Decision: the v2 launch was NOT approved for Q3.
It is deferred and will proceed only if the accessibility audit is signed off by September 5; if the audit is not signed off by that date, the launch slips to Q4.
Sam flagged three open WCAG issues that must close first.
Marketing: The teaser campaign is approved to start September 1 regardless of the launch decision, framed as 'coming soon.'
Next sync: Aug 26.
Priya to circulate the audit checklist.

question: How long is the battery covered under the warranty?
chunks: Limited Warranty - TerraBike X1
Coverage: TerraBike warrants the X1 against defects in materials and workmanship for 36 months from the date of purchase to the original owner.
This covers the frame, motor, controller, and display.
Wear components: Certain parts are considered consumable and carry shorter coverage.
Batteries, as consumable components, carry a limited 12-month warranty, notwithstanding the 36-month coverage on the main unit.
Tires, brake pads, and chains are covered for 90 days against defects only.
The frame itself carries an extended lifetime structural warranty to the original owner.
Exclusions: damage from misuse, accidents, unauthorized modification, commercial use, or water immersion beyond the IP65 rating is not covered.
Normal cosmetic wear is excluded.
Claims: contact support with proof of purchase and the serial number.
Approved warranty repairs are free of parts and labor; shipping to the service center is the owner's responsibility.
Replacement parts may be new or refurbished.
This warranty is non-transferable.

question: What is the deductible for water damage?
chunks: HomeShield Renters Insurance - Policy Summary (Plan R2)
Your policy covers personal property against fire, theft, vandalism, and accidental water damage, up to the $30,000 contents limit.
The standard deductible is $500 per claim.
However, claims for water damage carry a separate, higher deductible of $2,500 per occurrence, reflecting their frequency.
Electronics are sub-limited to $3,000 per item unless individually scheduled.
Flood is not covered under this policy and is explicitly excluded; flood protection is available only by purchasing the separate National Flood rider.
Earthquake is likewise excluded.
Claims must generally be filed within 60 days of the loss.
Theft claims are an exception: they must be reported to police within 72 hours and filed with HomeShield within 30 days, or they may be denied.
Liability coverage of $100,000 is included for guest injury.
Premiums are billed monthly; a 12-day grace period applies before lapse.
Cancelling mid-term incurs a $25 short-rate fee.

question: What is the uptime SLA for the Free plan?
chunks: Nimbus Cloud - Plan & SLA Terms
Nimbus offers four plans: Free, Team, Business, and Enterprise.
The Free plan is best-effort with no uptime SLA.
Paid plans carry uptime commitments: Team 99.5%, Business 99.9%, Enterprise 99.95%, with service credits for breaches.
Seats: Business and Enterprise allow unlimited seats.
The Team plan is capped at 25 seats; beyond that you must upgrade.
The Free plan allows 3 seats.
Support response targets: Enterprise 1 hour, Business 4 hours, Team next business day, Free community-only.
Data handling: after account cancellation, customer data is retained for 90 days and then permanently deleted, allowing reactivation.
For customers in the EU, data is retained for only 30 days after cancellation to comply with GDPR minimization.
Backups run hourly and are kept 35 days.
API access is included on all paid plans.

question: Are workshop fees refundable?
chunks: DevConf 2026 - Attendee Terms
Registration includes access to all keynotes, talks, and the expo hall.
Hands-on workshops require separate registration and have limited capacity.
Refunds: conference passes are fully refundable up to 14 days before the event, and 50% refundable after that until 3 days prior.
Workshop fees, however, are non-refundable once purchased.
No refunds are issued for no-shows.
Ticket transfers: a conference pass may be transferred to another person up to 7 days before the event.
Only one transfer per ticket is permitted, and workshop seats cannot be transferred.
Recording: attendees may not record talks.
Keynotes are the exception - they may be photographed and recorded for personal, non-commercial use.
Press require a separate media credential.
Code of conduct applies to all attendees; badges must be worn at all times in the venue.
